types.name: FeaturesEnableDisable
types.description: Control flags for enabling or disabling specific image transformation features

enumValues.name: UPSCALE
enumValues.description: Controls whether images can be enlarged beyond their original dimensions. When disabled, prevents upscaling to maintain image quality. When enabled, allows enlargement which may reduce quality but provides flexibility for responsive design.

types.name: FilterAvailableAttributeInput
types.description:
Filtering criteria for retrieving available product attributes.

Enables filtering the list of available attributes based on their properties such as visibility, searchability, and data types. Used to discover which attributes can be used for product filtering and search.

inputFields.name: isPublic
inputFields.description:
Filter attributes by public visibility status.

When true, returns only attributes that are publicly visible to customers.
When false, returns only attributes that are private or internal.
When not specified, returns attributes regardless of visibility status.

inputFields.name: isSearchable
inputFields.description:
Filter attributes by searchability status.

When true, returns only attributes that can be used in search queries.
When false, returns only attributes that are not searchable.
When not specified, returns attributes regardless of searchability status.

inputFields.name: ids
inputFields.description:
Filter attributes by specific IDs.

List of attribute IDs to include in the results. When provided, only attributes with matching IDs will be returned.

inputFields.name: types
inputFields.description:
Filter attributes by data types.

List of attribute data types to include in the results. When provided, only attributes with matching data types will be returned.

types.name: Firebase
types.description:
Firebase-specific authentication information and metadata.

  Contains Firebase authentication service data including provider information, multi-factor authentication details, and identity mappings.

fields.name: identities
fields.description:
Identity provider mappings and user identifiers.

    Contains mappings between the user's identity and various authentication providers, including provider-specific user identifiers.

fields.name: sign_in_provider
fields.description:
Primary authentication provider used for sign-in.

    Identifies which authentication method was used for the user's current session (e.g., 'password', 'google.com', 'facebook.com').

fields.name: sign_in_second_factor
fields.description:
Second factor authentication method used during sign-in.

    When multi-factor authentication is enabled, indicates the type of second factor used (e.g., 'phone', 'totp').

fields.name: second_factor_identifier
fields.description:
Identifier for the second factor authentication method.

    Unique identifier for the specific second factor device or method used during authentication.

fields.name: tenant
fields.description:
Authentication environment identifier.

    Identifies the specific authentication environment or tenant context for this user session.

types.name: Fit
types.description: Controls how images are resized and positioned within specified dimensions while maintaining aspect ratio. Only effective when both width and height are specified.

enumValues.name: BOUNDS
enumValues.description: Scales the image to fit entirely within the specified dimensions, preserving aspect ratio. May result in letterboxing or pillarboxing if aspect ratios don't match.

enumValues.name: COVER
enumValues.description: Scales the image to completely fill the specified dimensions, preserving aspect ratio. May crop parts of the image if aspect ratios don't match.

enumValues.name: CROP
enumValues.description: Resizes and crops the image from the center to exactly match the specified dimensions, potentially removing content from edges.

types.name: Format
types.description: Output format for the transformed image, determining compression method, quality, and browser compatibility

enumValues.name: AUTO
enumValues.description: Automatically selects the optimal format based on browser capabilities, image content, and transformation requirements

enumValues.name: AVIF
enumValues.description: AV1 Image File Format - next-generation format with superior compression and quality, supported by modern browsers

enumValues.name: BJPG
enumValues.description: Baseline JPEG format optimized for broad compatibility and sequential loading

enumValues.name: GIF
enumValues.description: Graphics Interchange Format supporting animation and transparency, ideal for simple graphics and animations

enumValues.name: JPG
enumValues.description: Standard JPEG format with lossy compression, best for photographs and complex images

enumValues.name: JXL
enumValues.description: JPEG XL format offering better compression than JPEG with both lossy and lossless modes

enumValues.name: MP4
enumValues.description: MP4 video format (H.264) for converting animated images to video

enumValues.name: PJPG
enumValues.description: Progressive JPEG that loads in multiple passes, improving perceived loading speed

enumValues.name: PJXL
enumValues.description: Progressive JPEG XL that loads incrementally for better user experience

enumValues.name: PNG
enumValues.description: Portable Network Graphics with lossless compression, ideal for images with transparency or sharp edges

enumValues.name: PNG8
enumValues.description: 8-bit PNG with 256-color palette and transparency, smaller file size than full PNG

enumValues.name: WEBP
enumValues.description: WebP format providing excellent compression for both lossy and lossless images

enumValues.name: WEBPLL
enumValues.description: WebP lossless format maintaining perfect image quality with better compression than PNG

enumValues.name: WEBPLY
enumValues.description: WebP lossy format offering better compression than JPEG while maintaining quality

types.name: GCIPMetadata
types.description:
User account metadata and activity timestamps.

  Contains important timestamps related to user account activity, authentication events, and session management.

fields.name: lastSignInTime
fields.description:
Timestamp of the user's most recent successful authentication.

    Indicates when the user last signed in to their account, used for security monitoring and session management.

fields.name: creationTime
fields.description:
Timestamp when the user account was originally created.

    Records the initial account creation time, used for account age tracking and audit purposes.

fields.name: lastRefreshTime
fields.description:
Timestamp of the most recent token refresh operation.

    Indicates when the user's authentication tokens were last refreshed, used for session lifecycle management.

types.name: GCIPProviderData
types.description:
Authentication provider-specific user information.

  Contains user profile data and identifiers from external authentication providers like Google, Facebook, or other OAuth providers.

fields.name: uid
fields.description:
User identifier from the authentication provider.

    The unique ID assigned to the user by the external authentication provider.

fields.name: providerId
fields.description:
Authentication provider identifier.

    Indicates which external service provided the authentication (e.g., 'google.com', 'facebook.com').

fields.name: displayName
fields.description:
User's display name from the authentication provider.

    The name associated with the user's account on the external authentication provider.

fields.name: photoUrl
fields.description:
URL to the user's profile photo from the authentication provider.

    Link to the user's avatar or profile picture hosted by the external authentication provider.

fields.name: federatedId
fields.description:
Federated identifier for the user from the authentication provider.

    Provider-specific identifier used for federated identity management and account linking.

fields.name: email
fields.description:
User's email address from the authentication provider.

    The email address associated with the user's account on the external authentication provider.

fields.name: rawId
fields.description:
Raw user identifier from the authentication provider.

    Unprocessed user ID as provided by the external authentication service.

fields.name: screenName
fields.description:
User's screen name or username from the authentication provider.

    The public username or handle associated with the user's account on the external provider.

fields.name: phoneNumber
fields.description:
User's phone number from the authentication provider.

    Phone number associated with the user's account on the external authentication provider.

types.name: GCIPUser
types.description:
Authenticated user session with comprehensive profile and token information.

  Represents a complete user authentication session from Google Cloud Identity Platform including profile data, authentication tokens, provider information, and security settings. Used for maintaining user sessions and accessing protected resources.

fields.name: uid
fields.description:
Unique user identifier for authentication and authorization.

    Permanent identifier for the user account, either supplied during user creation or automatically generated. Used throughout the system for user identification and session management.

fields.name: email
fields.description:
Primary email address for the user account.

    Email address used for authentication, communication, and account recovery. Must be unique across the system and serves as the primary login identifier.

fields.name: emailVerified
fields.description:
Email verification status for account security.

    Indicates whether the user has verified ownership of their email address through the verification process. Affects account security and feature access.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description:
User's display name for personalization.

    Human-readable name shown in user interfaces and communications. Can be different from the email address and is used for personalized user experiences.

fields.name: photoUrl
fields.description:
Profile photo URL for user avatar display.

    URL pointing to the user's profile picture, used for avatar display in user interfaces and social features.

fields.name: phoneNumber
fields.description:
Phone number for two-factor authentication and communication.

    User's phone number used for SMS-based authentication, account recovery, and optional communication preferences.

fields.name: disabled
fields.description:
Account status indicating if the user has been disabled.

    When true, the user account is disabled and cannot be used for authentication. Used for account suspension and security management.

fields.name: isAnonymous
fields.description:
Anonymous session indicator for guest users.

    Indicates whether this is an anonymous user session without full authentication. Anonymous users have limited access to system features.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description:
User account metadata and activity timestamps.

    Contains important timestamps related to user account activity, authentication events, and session management for auditing and security purposes.

fields.name: tokensValidAfterTime
fields.description:
Token validity threshold timestamp.

    All tokens issued before this time are considered invalid. Used for security purposes when user credentials are compromised or changed.

fields.name: tenantId
fields.description:
Authentication environment identifier.

    Identifies the specific authentication environment or tenant context for this user account.

fields.name: providerData
fields.description:
External authentication providers linked to this user.

    List of external authentication services (Google, Facebook, etc.) that can be used to authenticate this user account.

fields.name: passwordHash
fields.description:
Encrypted password hash for security.

    Cryptographically hashed password used for authentication. Never exposed in plain text for security reasons.

fields.name: passwordSalt
fields.description:
Password encryption salt for enhanced security.

    Random data used in password hashing to prevent rainbow table attacks and enhance password security.

fields.name: authDomain
fields.description:
Authentication domain for the user account.

    Domain context for user authentication, used for multi-domain authentication scenarios.

fields.name: lastLoginAt
fields.description:
Timestamp of the user's most recent login.

    Records when the user last successfully authenticated, used for security monitoring and session tracking.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description:
User account creation timestamp.

    Records when the user account was originally created in the authentication system.

fields.name: accessToken
fields.description:
JWT access token for API authentication.

    Short-lived token used to authenticate API requests and access protected resources. Contains user claims and permissions for authorization decisions.

fields.name: refreshToken
fields.description:
Refresh token for session renewal.

    Long-lived token used to obtain new access tokens when they expire, enabling persistent user sessions without requiring re-authentication.

fields.name: expirationTime
fields.description:
Access token expiration timestamp.

    Date and time when the current access token expires and needs to be refreshed. Used for automatic token renewal and session management.

fields.name: multiFactor
fields.description:
Multi-factor authentication configuration and status.

    Contains information about the user's MFA setup including enrolled factors, backup codes, and authentication preferences for enhanced account security.

enumValues.description: Male

enumValues.name: F

types.name: IAttributeDecimalRangeFilter
types.description:
Decimal range boundaries for numeric attribute filtering.

Defines the minimum and maximum decimal values found in search results for a numeric attribute, used for building precise range slider filters and decimal input controls.

fields.name: min
fields.description:
Minimum decimal value found for this attribute in current results.

The lowest decimal value present in the search results for this attribute, used as the lower bound for precise range filtering controls.

fields.name: max
fields.description:
Maximum decimal value found for this attribute in current results.

The highest decimal value present in the search results for this attribute, used as the upper bound for precise range filtering controls.

types.name: IAttributeFilter
types.description:
Filterable attribute with available values and ranges for product search.

Represents a product attribute that can be used for filtering search results, containing all available values and ranges found in the current result set. Used for building faceted search interfaces and attribute-based product filtering.

fields.name: id
fields.description:
Unique identifier of the product attribute.

System-generated ID that uniquely identifies this attribute across the catalog, used for referencing the attribute in filters and search operations.

fields.name: type
fields.description:
Data type of the attribute values.

Specifies the format for attribute values, determining how the attribute should be processed, displayed, and filtered in search interfaces.

fields.name: textFilters
fields.description:
Available text-based filter values for this attribute.

Collection of distinct text values found in the current search results for this attribute, along with their occurrence counts. Used for building checkbox or list-based filters in search interfaces.

fields.name: integerRangeFilter
fields.description:
Numeric range boundaries for integer-type attributes.

Minimum and maximum integer values found in the current search results for this attribute, used for building range slider or numeric input filters.

fields.name: decimalRangeFilter
fields.description:
Numeric range boundaries for decimal-type attributes.

Minimum and maximum decimal values found in the current search results for this attribute, used for building precise range filters for measurements and prices.

types.name: IAttributeIntegerRangeFilter
types.description:
Integer range boundaries for numeric attribute filtering.

Defines the minimum and maximum integer values found in search results for a numeric attribute, used for building range slider filters and numeric input controls.

fields.name: min
fields.description:
Minimum integer value found for this attribute in current results.

The lowest integer value present in the search results for this attribute, used as the lower bound for range filtering controls.

fields.name: max
fields.description:
Maximum integer value found for this attribute in current results.

The highest integer value present in the search results for this attribute, used as the upper bound for range filtering controls.

types.name: IAttributeTextFilter
types.description:
Text-based attribute filter value with product counts and selection state.

Represents a specific text value for an attribute that can be used for filtering products, including occurrence counts and selection status for building interactive filter interfaces.

fields.name: value
fields.description:
Attribute value text for filtering.

The actual text value of the attribute that customers can select to filter products. This is the display value shown in filter interfaces.

fields.name: count
fields.description:
Number of products matching this value with current filters applied.

Count of products that have this attribute value and also match all currently applied search filters and criteria. Used for showing relevant filter options.

fields.name: countTotal
fields.description:
Total number of products with this value across all results.

Count of products that have this attribute value without any filters applied, representing the complete availability of this value in the catalog.

fields.name: countActive
fields.description:
Number of products with this value excluding other attribute filters.

Count of products that have this attribute value with all other attribute filters removed but keeping search terms and category filters. Used for showing filter impact.

fields.name: isSelected
fields.description:
Selection status of this attribute value in current search.

Indicates whether this attribute value is currently selected as an active filter in the customer's search criteria.

types.name: IBaseTemplate
types.description:
Base interface for all message templates in the system.

  Defines common properties and functionality shared between email templates and document templates, including content management, localization, data integration, and metadata tracking.

fields.name: id
fields.description:
Unique identifier for the message template.

Used to reference and manage specific templates throughout the messaging system for emails, documents, and notifications.

fields.name: contents
fields.description:
Handlebars template content for each supported language.

The actual template markup using Handlebars syntax for dynamic content generation, stored per language for localization support.

fields.name: names
fields.description:
Localized template names for different languages.

Human-readable names for the template in various languages, used for template identification and management in multilingual environments.

fields.name: content
fields.description:
Compiled template content for rendering.

The processed template content ready for variable substitution and message generation, derived from the localized contents based on the target language.

fields.name: customQuery
fields.description:
GraphQL query for additional data enrichment.

Custom query executed before template rendering to fetch supplementary data not included in the original event payload, enabling rich template content.

fields.name: queryVariables
fields.description:
Variables for custom GraphQL query execution.

JSON object containing variables used in the custom query, supports Handlebars expressions for dynamic variable generation based on event data.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description:
Template creation timestamp.

Date and time when this template was initially created in the system, used for auditing and template lifecycle management.

fields.name: lastModifiedAt
fields.description:
Template last modification timestamp.

Date and time when any template content or configuration was last updated, used for change tracking and version control.

fields.name: createdBy
fields.description:
Template creator user identifier.

ID of the user who originally created this template, used for ownership tracking and access control.

fields.name: lastModifiedBy
fields.description:
Template last editor user identifier.

ID of the user who most recently modified this template, used for change attribution and audit trails.

types.name: IBusinessRuleExpression
types.description:
Base interface for all business rule expression types defining common properties and behavior.

Expressions are the fundamental building blocks for business rule conditions and calculations.
Each expression type provides specific operators and validation logic for different data types including strings, numbers, dates, arrays, and complex aggregations.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this expression within the business rule system

fields.name: type
fields.description: Data type classification of the expression determining available operators and validation rules

types.name: IBusinessRuleNode
types.description:
Base interface for all business rule node types defining common properties and behavior.

Nodes are the fundamental processing units within a business rule graph, each serving a specific function:
- Input nodes receive and validate incoming data
- Decision table nodes contain the core business logic and rules
- Output nodes format and return the final results
- Function nodes execute custom computational logic

fields.name: type
fields.description: Functional classification of the node determining its role in the business rule execution flow

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the node within the business rule graph

fields.name: name
fields.description: Human-readable name of the node for identification and documentation purposes

fields.name: description
fields.description: Detailed explanation of the node's purpose and functionality within the business rule workflow

types.name: IClusterDrillDown
types.description:
Drill-down navigation configuration for cluster product filtering.

Defines how customers can navigate through cluster products using attribute-based filtering. Each drill-down represents a filterable attribute that helps customers narrow down product choices within a cluster.

fields.name: attributeId
fields.description:
Unique identifier of the attribute used for drill-down filtering.

References the specific product attribute that customers can use to filter and navigate through cluster products, such as color, size, or material.

fields.name: priority
fields.description:
Display order priority for drill-down navigation.

Numeric value that determines the sequence in which drill-down filters appear in customer interfaces. Lower values appear first in the navigation flow.

fields.name: displayType
fields.description:
User interface display method for the drill-down filter.

Specifies how customers interact with this drill-down filter, such as through radio buttons, dropdowns, image selection, or color swatches.

types.name: IClusterOption
types.description:
Configurable option within a product cluster for customer selection.

Represents a selectable option that customers can choose when configuring a cluster product, such as size, color, or variant. Each option contains a set of products and configuration settings that define customer choices.

fields.name: id
fields.description:
Unique identifier for the cluster option.

System-generated ID

fields.name: clusterOptionId
fields.description:
Cluster option unique identifier.

System-generated ID used for referencing this specific option within cluster configurations and customer selections.

fields.name: isRequired
fields.description:
Requirement status for customer selection.

When 'Y', customers must select a product from this option to complete their configuration.
When 'N', this option is optional and customers can skip it.

fields.name: hidden
fields.description:
Visibility status for customer interfaces.

When 'Y', this option is hidden from customers and not displayed in configuration interfaces.
When 'N', this option is visible and available for customer selection.

fields.name: defaultProduct
fields.description:
Default product selection for this option.

Product that is pre-selected when customers first view this option, providing a recommended or the first choice to streamline the configuration process.

fields.name: products
fields.description:
Available products for customer selection within this option.

Collection of products that customers can choose from when configuring this cluster option. Each product represents a different variant or choice within the option category.

fields.name: names
fields.description:
Localized names for the cluster option in different languages.

Collection of option names translated into various languages for international support and multilingual storefronts. Used for displaying option labels to customers.

fields.name: descriptions
fields.description:
Detailed descriptions of the cluster option in multiple languages.

Comprehensive option descriptions providing detailed information about what this option represents and how it affects the final product configuration.

fields.name: shortDescriptions
fields.description:
Brief summaries of the cluster option in different languages.

Concise option descriptions used in configuration interfaces where space is limited, providing quick explanations of the option's purpose and impact.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: Creation date

fields.name: lastModifiedAt
fields.description: Last modified date

types.name: IEventActionConfig
types.description:
Base interface for event action configurations.

Defines the common structure for all event action types including email and webhook configurations. Provides shared fields for identification, timing, and event triggering.

fields.name: id
fields.description:
Unique identifier for the event action configuration.

Uses UUID v7 format for chronological ordering and global uniqueness across the system.

fields.name: trigger
fields.description:
System event that activates this action configuration.

Defines which event type will cause this automation to execute, enabling event-driven workflow processing.

fields.name: code
fields.description:
Custom event identifier for CUSTOM_EVENT triggers.

Required when trigger type is CUSTOM_EVENT. Used to match and execute specific configurations when custom events are triggered.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description:
Timestamp when the event action configuration was initially created.

Provides audit trail information for tracking configuration lifecycle and changes.

fields.name: lastModifiedAt
fields.description:
Timestamp when the event action configuration was last updated.

Provides audit trail information for tracking recent changes and configuration history.

fields.name: createdBy
fields.description:
User identifier who created this event action configuration.

Provides audit trail information for tracking configuration ownership and accountability.

fields.name: lastModifiedBy
fields.description:
User identifier who last modified this event action configuration.

Provides audit trail information for tracking recent changes and accountability.

fields.name: names
fields.description:
Localized display names for the event action configuration.

Provides human-readable names in multiple languages for user interface display and identification purposes.

args.name: language
args.description:
Language code filter for localized content.

Filters localized string results to return only entries matching the specified language code. When omitted, returns all available language variants.

fields.name: channelIds
fields.description:
Array of channel identifiers for filtering event action configurations.

Used to restrict event action configurations to specific communication channels. When omitted, returns all channels.

types.name: ImageCsvInput
types.description: Input for bulk importing images from a CSV file with field mapping configuration

inputFields.name: file
inputFields.description:
CSV file containing image data for bulk import.

    Must be a valid CSV file with headers that match the configured field mappings. Each row represents one image to be created with the specified metadata and file references.

inputFields.name: mappings
inputFields.description:
Configuration mapping CSV columns to image entity fields.

    Defines how CSV column headers correspond to image fields. If not provided, the system will attempt to auto-map based on standard column names. Each mapping must be unique and valid.

types.name: ImageCsvMapping
types.description: Mapping configuration between CSV column headers and image entity fields

inputFields.name: csvHeader
inputFields.description:
Name of the column header in the CSV file.

    Must exactly match the column header in the uploaded CSV file. Used to identify which CSV column contains the data for the corresponding image field.

inputFields.name: fieldName
inputFields.description:
Target image field that will receive the CSV column data.

    Specifies which image entity field should be populated with the data from the corresponding CSV column. Each field has specific validation requirements and data format expectations.

types.name: ImageFieldName
types.description: Fields available for image CSV mapping.

enumValues.description: Preserve copyright notice, creator, credit line, licensor, and web statement of rights fields.

inputFields.description: Friendly name for your image transformation

inputFields.description: Image transformation options

types.name: ImageVariant
types.description: A transformed version of an image with specific modifications applied

fields.name: name
fields.description:
Human-readable name for the image variant.

    Typically describes the transformation applied or the intended use case (e.g., 'thumbnail', 'hero-banner', 'mobile-optimized').

fields.name: language
fields.description:
Language code associated with this image variant.

    Indicates the language context for localized image content or text overlays. Uses standard language codes like 'EN', 'DE', 'FR'.

fields.name: url
fields.description:
Direct URL to access the transformed image.

    Fully qualified URL that can be used to display or download the transformed image. The URL includes all applied transformations and optimizations.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description:
MIME type of the transformed image.

    Indicates the file format of the transformed image (e.g., 'image/jpeg', 'image/webp', 'image/png'). May differ from the original image format based on transformations applied.

types.name: IncentiveActions
types.description: Available actions that can be executed by incentive business rules to provide customer benefits and promotional offers

enumValues.name: ADD_PRODUCT_TO_BONUS_ITEMS
enumValues.description: Add a specific product to the order as a bonus item without charge. Used for promotional giveaways and product bundling.

enumValues.name: GIVE_DISCOUNT_TO_CHEAPEST_ITEM
enumValues.description: Apply a discount to the lowest-priced item in the order. Common for 'buy X get cheapest free' promotions.

enumValues.name: GIVE_DISCOUNT_TO_ORDER_TOTAL
enumValues.description: Apply a discount to the entire order total. Used for percentage or fixed amount discounts on the complete purchase.

enumValues.name: SET_SHIPPING_COSTS
enumValues.description: Override shipping costs with a specific amount. Used for free shipping promotions or special shipping rates.

types.name: IncentiveDiscountType
types.description: Calculation methods for applying discount values in incentive business rules

enumValues.name: FlatFee
enumValues.description: Fixed amount discount applied as an absolute monetary value

enumValues.name: Percentage
enumValues.description: Proportional discount applied as a percentage of the target value

types.name: Inventory
types.description:
Inventory record representing stock levels and location details for products.

  Contains comprehensive inventory information including stock quantities, supplier details and warehouse locations.

fields.name: id
fields.description:
Unique identifier for the inventory record.

    Primary key used to reference this specific inventory entry throughout the system and for inventory operations.

fields.name: productId
fields.description:
Product identifier associated with this inventory.

    References the specific product that this inventory record tracks, linking stock levels to product catalog entries.

fields.name: quantity
fields.description:
Current stock quantity available.

    The number of units currently in stock at this location. Used for availability calculations and stock level monitoring.

fields.name: costPrice
fields.description:
Cost price per unit for this inventory batch.

    The cost incurred to acquire each unit in this inventory batch, used for margin calculations and inventory valuation.

fields.name: supplier
fields.description:
Supplier name for this inventory batch.

    The company or organization that supplied the products in this inventory record, used for supplier management and procurement tracking.

fields.name: supplierCode
fields.description:
Supplier's code for cross-referencing.

    The supplier's own identifier used for ordering, communication, and inventory reconciliation with supplier systems.

fields.name: sku
fields.description:
Stock Keeping Unit for inventory tracking.

    Unique product code used for inventory management and product identification across all warehouse operations.

fields.name: dateModified
fields.description:
[Deprecated] Last modification timestamp for the inventory record.

    Date and time when this inventory record was last updated, used for change tracking and synchronization.

fields.name: warehouseId
fields.description:
Warehouse identifier where the inventory is located.

    References the specific warehouse or storage facility where this inventory is physically stored.

fields.name: companyId
fields.description:
Company identifier for inventory ownership.

    References the company this inventory is reserved for, used for multi-tenant inventory management and reporting.

fields.name: location
fields.description:
Specific location within the warehouse.

    Detailed location information such as aisle, shelf, or bin number for precise inventory placement and picking operations.

fields.name: nextDeliveryDate
fields.description:
Estimated date for next inventory replenishment.

    Expected delivery date for the next shipment of this product, used for stock planning and customer communication about availability.

fields.name: notes
fields.description:
Additional notes and comments about this inventory.

    Free-form text for storing special handling instructions, quality notes, or other relevant information about this inventory batch.

types.name: InventoryCsvInput
types.description:
Input for importing inventory records from a CSV file.

  Contains the CSV file data and optional field mappings to define how CSV columns correspond to inventory properties. Used for bulk inventory import operations with customizable field mapping.

inputFields.name: file
inputFields.description:
CSV file containing inventory data.

    The uploaded CSV file must contain valid inventory data with proper headers. File size is limited and must be in CSV format with comma-separated values.

inputFields.name: mappings
inputFields.description:
Field mapping configuration for CSV import.

    Optional array of mappings that define how CSV column headers correspond to inventory fields. If not provided, default mappings will be used based on standard column names.

types.name: InventoryCsvMapping
types.description:
Mapping configuration for CSV column to inventory field association.

  Defines how CSV file columns should be mapped to inventory record fields during import operations. Each mapping specifies which CSV column header corresponds to which inventory property.

inputFields.name: csvHeader
inputFields.description:
CSV column header name.

    The exact name of the column header in the CSV file that contains the data for this inventory field. Must match the header exactly as it appears in the CSV file.

inputFields.name: fieldName
inputFields.description:
Target inventory field for this CSV column.

    Specifies which inventory property this CSV column should populate during import. The field name determines data validation rules and processing requirements.

types.name: InventoryDeleteResponse
types.description: Response returned if delete inventory was successful.

fields.name: messages
fields.description: [DEPRECATED]

enumValues.name: LOOKUP_KEY
enumValues.description:
[Required][String] Unique identifier for inventory record retrieval.

Used to locate and reference specific inventory entries in the system for updates, queries, and reporting operations., must be one of the following: [id, sku, supplierCode, sourceId]

enumValues.name: INVENTORY_TYPE
enumValues.description:
[Required][Enum] Classification of inventory storage and management type.

Determines how the inventory is managed, whether it's held locally in company warehouses or managed by external suppliers for drop-shipping., must be one of the following: [local, supplier]

enumValues.name: QUANTITY
enumValues.description:
[Required][Integer] Current stock level available for this inventory record.

The number of units physically available at this location, used for availability calculations and stock level monitoring.

enumValues.name: SKU
enumValues.description:
[Optional][String] Stock Keeping Unit for product identification.

Unique alphanumeric code used for inventory tracking, order processing, and product identification across all warehouse operations., required only when lookup key is sku

enumValues.name: SUPPLIER_CODE
enumValues.description:
[Optional][String] Supplier's product identification code.

The supplier's own product identifier used for ordering, inventory reconciliation, and communication with supplier systems., required only when lookup key is supplierCode

enumValues.name: SUPPLIER
enumValues.description:
[Optional][String] Supplier name for this inventory batch.

The company or organization that supplied the products. Defaults:
- 'INTERN' for locally managed inventory
- Product supplier name for supplier-managed inventory, required only when lookup key is supplierCode

enumValues.name: PRODUCT_ID
enumValues.description:
[Required][Integer] Product identifier linked to this inventory.

References the specific product in the catalog that this inventory record tracks, enabling product-inventory relationships., required only when lookup key is id

enumValues.name: SOURCE_ID
enumValues.description:
[Optional][String] Data source identifier for inventory integration.

Unique identifier for the system or process that created or manages this inventory record, used for data lineage and synchronization., required only when lookup key is sourceId

enumValues.name: SOURCE_NAME
enumValues.description:
[Optional][String] Data source name for inventory tracking.

Name of the system, supplier, or process that provided this inventory data, used for integration management and data quality., required only when lookup key is sourceId

enumValues.name: WAREHOUSE_ID
enumValues.description:
[Optional][Integer] Warehouse facility identifier.

References the specific warehouse or storage facility where this inventory is physically located for fulfillment operations.

enumValues.name: COMPANY_ID
enumValues.description:
[Optional][Integer] Company identifier for inventory ownership.

References the company that owns or manages this inventory, used for multi-tenant inventory management and reporting.

enumValues.name: COMPANY_SOURCE_ID
enumValues.description:
[Optional][String] Company data source identifier.

Identifier for the system that provided the company information, used for company data synchronization and integration.

enumValues.name: COMPANY_SOURCE_NAME
enumValues.description:
[Optional][String] Company data source name.

Name of the system that provided the company data, used for data lineage tracking and integration management.

enumValues.name: LOCATION
enumValues.description:
[Optional][String] Specific storage location within the warehouse.

Detailed location information such as aisle, shelf, or bin number for precise inventory placement and efficient picking operations. Maximum 35 characters.

enumValues.name: COST_PRICE
enumValues.description:
[Optional][Decimal] Unit cost for this inventory batch.

The cost incurred to acquire each unit in this inventory record, used for margin calculations, inventory valuation, and profitability analysis.

enumValues.name: NEXT_DELIVERY_DATE
enumValues.description:
[Optional][DateTime] Expected replenishment delivery date.

Estimated date when the next shipment of this product will arrive, used for stock planning and customer availability communication. Must be in ISO8601 format. in ISO 8601 format 'YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sssZ' (e.g., 2024-10-31T09:55:00.000Z)

enumValues.name: NOTES
enumValues.description:
[Optional][String] Additional information and handling instructions.

Free-form text for storing special handling requirements, quality notes, or other relevant information about this inventory batch. Maximum 255 characters.

fields.name: shortName

fields.name: eanCode
fields.description:
European Article Number for global identification.

Standardized number used for product identification, inventory management, and point-of-sale systems.

fields.name: manufacturer

Product manufacturer information.

Company or brand that produces the product, used for brand filtering, warranty information, and supplier relationships.

fields.name: supplier

Primary supplier information.

Company or organization that supplies this product, used for procurement, inventory management, and supplier relationships.

fields.name: supplierCode

Supplier's product identification code.

fields.name: status
fields.description:
Current product lifecycle status.

Indicates the product's current state in its lifecycle, affecting availability and visibility.

fields.name: statusExtra
fields.description:
Additional status classification.

Supplementary status information often linked to custom value sets for specific business requirements and workflows.

fields.name: barCode
fields.description:
Product barcode for scanning and identification.

Machine-readable code used for inventory management, checkout processes, and automated product identification.

fields.name: turnoverGroup
fields.description:
Financial classification for reporting.

Groups products for financial reporting and analysis, used in accounting systems and business intelligence.

fields.name: taxonomy
fields.description:
Product classification hierarchy.

Standardized product categorization system used for organization, reporting, and integration with external systems.

fields.name: priceGroup
fields.description:
Pricing strategy classification.

Groups products with similar pricing rules and strategies, used by pricing engines for consistent pricing logic.

fields.name: orderable
fields.description:
Product availability for ordering.

Indicates whether customers can currently place orders for this product through the storefront and ordering systems.

fields.name: returnable
fields.description:
Product return policy eligibility.

Indicates whether this product can be returned by customers according to the return policy and business rules.

fields.name: physical
fields.description:
Product type classification for fulfillment.

Indicates whether this is a physical product requiring shipping or a digital product/service like downloads, warranties, or services.

fields.name: package
fields.description:
Product packaging type classification.

Describes how the product is packaged for sale (e.g., PIECE, BOX, BLISTER), affecting shipping, storage, and display.

fields.name: packageUnit
fields.description:
Individual item description within package.

Describes what constitutes one unit within the package (e.g., PIECE, BOX), used for inventory and ordering clarity.

fields.name: packageUnitQuantity
fields.description:
Quantity of individual items per package.

Number of individual units contained within one package, used for inventory calculations and customer information.

fields.name: minimumQuantity
fields.description:
Minimum order quantity for customers.

Smallest quantity customers can order, used to enforce business rules and ensure order profitability.

fields.name: unit
fields.description:
Base unit of measure for customer ordering.

Standard unit in which customers can order this product, used for pricing and quantity calculations.

fields.name: purchaseUnit
fields.description:
Unit of measure for supplier ordering.

Unit used when ordering from suppliers, which may differ from customer ordering units for bulk purchasing efficiency.

fields.name: purchaseMinimumQuantity
fields.description:
Minimum order quantity for supplier purchases.

Smallest quantity that can be ordered from suppliers, used for procurement planning and inventory management.

fields.name: economicOrderQuantity
fields.description:
Optimal order quantity for cost efficiency.

Quantity that provides the best balance of ordering costs and inventory holding costs, used for purchasing recommendations.

fields.name: orderableFrom
fields.description:
Start date for product ordering availability.

Date from which customers can begin placing orders for this product, used for product launches and seasonal availability.

fields.name: orderableTo
fields.description:
End date for product ordering availability.

Date after which customers can no longer place orders for this product, used for product discontinuation and seasonal restrictions.

fields.name: releaseDate
fields.description:
Product launch date for availability planning.

Date when the product was or will be officially released to the market, used for launch planning and availability scheduling.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description:
Product record creation timestamp.

Date and time when this product was first added to the system, used for auditing and product lifecycle tracking.

fields.name: lastModifiedAt
fields.description:
Product record last modification timestamp.

Date and time when any product information was last updated, used for change tracking and synchronization.

    Indicates the file format (e.g., 'application/pdf', 'application/zip', 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document'). Used for proper file handling and security validation.

fields.name: precompiled

Optimized template content for faster rendering.

Pre-processed and compiled version of the Handlebars template that improves rendering performance by eliminating compilation overhead during message generation.

types.name: Login
types.description:
Authentication response containing login session information.

  Returned after successful user authentication, providing session details and user information.

fields.name: providerId
fields.description:
Authentication provider identifier.

    Indicates which authentication provider was used for the login (e.g., 'google', 'facebook', 'email').

fields.name: operationType
fields.description:
Type of authentication operation performed.

    Describes the specific authentication action that was executed (e.g., 'signIn', 'signUp', 'anonymous').

fields.name: session
fields.description:
User session information.

    Contains the authenticated user's profile data, tokens, and session details. Null if authentication failed.

types.name: LoginInput
types.description:
User authentication credentials for login.

  Supports both email/password authentication and optional provider-based authentication.

inputFields.name: email
inputFields.description:
User's email address.

    Must be a valid email format and is used as the primary identifier for authentication.

inputFields.name: password
inputFields.description:
User's password.

    Must be at least 6 characters long. Password strength requirements may apply based on system configuration.

inputFields.name: provider
inputFields.description:
Optional authentication provider identifier.

    Used for third-party authentication providers like Google, Facebook, etc. If not specified, defaults to email/password authentication.

types.name: Logout
types.description:
Response for user logout operations (deprecated).

  This type is deprecated and will be replaced by a more comprehensive signOut response in future versions. Currently serves as a placeholder for logout functionality.

fields.name: todo
fields.description:
Placeholder field for future logout response data.

    This field is currently unused and will be replaced with meaningful logout response information in future versions.

types.name: MagicToken
types.description:
Magic token for passwordless authentication.

  Magic tokens provide a secure way to authenticate users without requiring passwords. They can be associated with either contacts or customers and support configurable expiration and usage limits.

fields.name: id
fields.description:
Unique identifier for the magic token.

    This UUID serves as the primary key and can be used to reference the token in authentication requests.

fields.name: contactId
fields.description:
Contact identifier associated with this magic token.

    When specified, the magic token will authenticate as this contact. Either contactId or customerId must be provided, but not both.

fields.name: customerId
fields.description:
Customer identifier associated with this magic token.

    When specified, the magic token will authenticate as this customer. Either customerId or contactId must be provided, but not both.

fields.name: expiresAt
fields.description:
Token expiration timestamp.

    After this date and time, the magic token becomes invalid and cannot be used for authentication. If null, the token never expires.

fields.name: lastAccessedAt
fields.description:
Timestamp of the last authentication attempt using this token.

    Updated each time the token is used for login, regardless of success or failure. Used for security monitoring and audit purposes.

fields.name: failedLogins
fields.description:
Count of failed authentication attempts using this token.

    Incremented each time an authentication attempt fails. Used for security monitoring and potential token blocking after too many failures.

fields.name: successfulLogins
fields.description:
Count of successful authentication attempts using this token.

    Incremented each time the token is successfully used for authentication. For one-time use tokens, this should never exceed 1.

fields.name: oneTimeUse
fields.description:
Whether this token can only be used once.

    When true, the token becomes invalid after the first successful authentication. When false, the token can be reused until it expires.

fields.name: extra
fields.description:
Additional metadata or context information for the token.

    Can store arbitrary string data related to the token's purpose, origin, or usage context. Useful for tracking and debugging.

inputFields.name: contactId
inputFields.description: Identifier of the associated contact, use either contactId or customerId

inputFields.name: customerId
inputFields.description: Identifier of the associated customer, use either customerId or contactId

inputFields.name: expiresAt
inputFields.description: Expiration date and time of the magic token

inputFields.name: oneTimeUse
inputFields.description: Indicates if the magic token is for one-time use only

types.name: MagicTokenResponse
types.description:
Paginated response containing magic tokens and pagination metadata.

Provides a structured response for magic token search operations with pagination support and result statistics.

fields.name: items
fields.description: List of items of type MagicToken

fields.description: Total number of items found

fields.name: offset
fields.description: The amount of items to show per page

fields.name: page
fields.description: The current page

fields.name: pages
fields.description: Total amount of pages

fields.name: start
fields.description: Start position of the current page

fields.name: end
fields.description: End position of the current page

types.name: MagicTokenSearchInput
types.description:
Search criteria for filtering magic tokens.

  Supports filtering by various token properties including associated users, expiration dates, usage statistics, and pagination options.

inputFields.name: ids
inputFields.description:
Filter by specific magic token IDs.

    Returns only tokens whose IDs match the provided list. Useful for retrieving specific tokens.

inputFields.name: contactIds
inputFields.description:
Filter by contact identifiers.

    Returns tokens associated with any of the specified contact IDs. Useful for finding all tokens belonging to specific contacts.

inputFields.name: customerIds
inputFields.description:
Filter by customer identifiers.

    Returns tokens associated with any of the specified customer IDs. Useful for finding all tokens belonging to specific customers.

inputFields.name: expiresAt
inputFields.description:
Filter by token expiration date range.

    Allows filtering tokens based on their expiration timestamps. Useful for finding expired tokens or tokens expiring within a specific timeframe.

inputFields.name: oneTimeUse
inputFields.description:
Filter by one-time use restriction.

    When true, returns only tokens that can be used once. When false, returns only reusable tokens. When null, returns all tokens regardless of usage restriction.

inputFields.name: failedLogins
inputFields.description:
Filter by failed login attempt count range.

    Allows filtering tokens based on the number of failed authentication attempts. Useful for security monitoring and identifying potentially compromised tokens.

inputFields.name: successfulLogins
inputFields.description:
Filter by successful login attempt count range.

    Allows filtering tokens based on the number of successful authentication attempts. Useful for usage analytics and identifying heavily used tokens.

inputFields.name: createdAt
inputFields.description:
Filter by token creation date range.

    Allows filtering tokens based on when they were originally created. Useful for finding tokens created within specific time periods.

inputFields.name: lastModifiedAt
inputFields.description:
Filter by last modification date range.

    Allows filtering tokens based on when they were last updated. Useful for finding recently modified tokens or tracking changes.

inputFields.name: page
inputFields.description:
Page number for pagination.

    Specifies which page of results to return. Must be 1 or greater.

inputFields.name: offset
inputFields.description:
Number of items per page.

    Controls how many magic tokens are returned in each page. Must be 1 or greater.

types.name: MagicTokenUpdateInput
types.description:
Parameters for updating an existing magic token's properties.

  Allows modification of magic token settings such as expiration time, usage restrictions, associated user, and metadata. All fields are optional - only provided fields will be updated.

inputFields.name: contactId
inputFields.description:
Contact identifier for reassociating the magic token.

    When specified, the token will authenticate as this contact instead of its current association. Cannot be used together with customerId. Must be a valid contact ID that exists in the system.

inputFields.name: customerId
inputFields.description:
Customer identifier for reassociating the magic token.

    When specified, the token will authenticate as this customer instead of its current association. Cannot be used together with contactId. Must be a valid customer ID that exists in the system.

inputFields.name: expiresAt
inputFields.description:
Updated expiration timestamp for the magic token.

    ISO 8601 formatted date and time when the token should expire. After this time, the token becomes invalid and cannot be used for authentication. If not provided, the current expiration remains unchanged.

inputFields.name: oneTimeUse
inputFields.description:
Updated one-time use restriction for the magic token.

    When true, the token becomes invalid after the first successful authentication. When false, the token can be reused until it expires. If not provided, the current setting remains unchanged.

inputFields.name: extra
inputFields.description:
Updated metadata or context information for the magic token.

    Array of strings containing arbitrary metadata related to the token's purpose, origin, or usage context. This completely replaces any existing extra data. Useful for tracking token usage and debugging.

types.name: Media
types.description: Unified media access point providing single items and paginated collections for all media types

fields.name: image
fields.description:
Single image media item.

    Provides access to a specific image with its metadata, transformations, and localized variants. Used for retrieving individual images by ID.

args.name: mediaId
args.description:
Unique identifier of the image to retrieve.

      Must be a valid media ID representing an existing image in the media storage system.

fields.name: images
fields.description:
Paginated collection of image media items.

    Returns a searchable and filterable list of images with pagination support. Includes metadata for navigation and result counts.

args.name: search
args.description:
Search criteria for filtering images.

      If not provided, returns all accessible images with default pagination settings. Supports filtering by metadata, dimensions, file types, and upload dates.

fields.name: video
fields.description:
Single video media item.

    Provides access to a specific video with its metadata, hosting information, and localized variants. Used for retrieving individual videos by ID.

args.name: mediaId
args.description:
Unique identifier of the video to retrieve.

      Must be a valid media ID representing an existing video in the media storage system.

fields.name: videos
fields.description:
Paginated collection of video media items.

    Returns a searchable and filterable list of videos with pagination support. Includes metadata for navigation and result counts.

args.name: search
args.description:
Search criteria for filtering videos.

      If not provided, returns all accessible videos with default pagination settings. Supports filtering by metadata, duration, resolution, file types, and upload dates.

fields.name: document
fields.description:
Single document media item.

    Provides access to a specific document with its metadata, file information, and localized variants. Used for retrieving individual documents by ID.

args.name: mediaId
args.description:
Unique identifier of the document to retrieve.

      Must be a valid media ID representing an existing document in the media storage system.

fields.name: documents
fields.description:
Paginated collection of document media items.

    Returns a searchable and filterable list of documents with pagination support. Includes metadata for navigation and result counts.

args.name: search
args.description:
Search criteria for filtering documents.

      If not provided, returns all accessible documents with default pagination settings. Supports filtering by metadata, file types, sizes, and upload dates.

fields.name: attachment
fields.description:
Retrieve a specific attachment by its identifier.

    Returns detailed information about an attachment including metadata, storage location, file information, and association details. Used for attachment display and management operations.

args.name: id
args.description:
Unique identifier of the attachment to retrieve.

      Must be a valid attachment ID representing an existing attachment in the media storage system.

fields.name: attachments
fields.description:
Search and retrieve attachments with comprehensive filtering and pagination.

    Provides flexible search capabilities across attachment metadata including names, file types, associations, and upload dates. Results are paginated and filtered based on user permissions for secure access control.

args.name: input
args.description:
Search criteria for filtering attachments.

      If not provided, returns all accessible attachments with default pagination settings. Supports filtering by metadata, file types, associations, and upload dates. Access is controlled based on user permissions.

types.name: MediaAttachment
types.description: Private attachment file with restricted access and ownership controls

fields.name: id
fields.description:
Globally unique identifier for the media item.

    Used to reference and retrieve the specific media item across all operations. This ID is immutable and unique across the entire media system.

fields.name: sparePartsMachineId
fields.description: Associates the media item with a specific spare parts machine for maintenance and service documentation

fields.name: alt
fields.description:
Alternative text descriptions for accessibility and SEO purposes.

    Provides brief, descriptive text that explains the media content for screen readers and when the media cannot be displayed. Essential for web accessibility compliance and search engine optimization.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
Detailed descriptions of the media content.

    Provides comprehensive information about what the media shows or contains. Used for content management, search indexing, and user information display.

fields.name: tags
fields.description:
Searchable tags for categorizing and organizing media content.

    Keywords that help classify and discover media items. Used for search functionality, content filtering, and organizational purposes.

fields.name: type
fields.description:
Type classification of the media item.

    Indicates whether this is an image, video, document, or attachment. Used for processing, display, and filtering logic.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description:
Timestamp when the media item was originally created.

    Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone information. Used for sorting, filtering, and audit purposes.

fields.name: lastModifiedAt
fields.description:
Timestamp when the media item was last modified.

    Updated whenever the media metadata or content is changed. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone information.

fields.name: priority
fields.description:
Display order priority for the media item.

    Lower numeric values indicate higher priority and will be displayed first. Used for controlling the order of media items in galleries, carousels, and listings.

fields.name: attachments
fields.description:
Collection of private attachment files in different languages and formats.

    Contains confidential or sensitive files that are restricted to specific users or organizations. Each attachment can have multiple language variants and format versions.

fields.name: orderId

Associates the attachment with a specific order.

    Links the attachment to order-related documentation such as invoices, contracts, or delivery notes. Cannot be used simultaneously with sparePartsMachineId as attachments are associated with either orders or machines.

fields.name: companyId
fields.description:
Associates the attachment with a specific company owner.

    Indicates that the attachment belongs to and is accessible by the specified company. Cannot be used simultaneously with customerId as attachments have a single owner type.

fields.name: customerId
fields.description:
Associates the attachment with a specific customer owner.

    Indicates that the attachment belongs to and is accessible by the specified customer. Cannot be used simultaneously with companyId as attachments have a single owner type.

inputFields.name: base64

    The complete file data encoded in base64 format. Maximum size is approximately 10MB (13,981,017 characters). This method is useful for small files or when direct file upload is not available.

inputFields.name: fileName
inputFields.description:

    Must include the file extension and be between 5-255 characters. This name will be used for storage and affects how the file is processed and referenced.
