types.name: OrderStatusesSearchInput
types.description: Search criteria and filters for retrieving order statuses with pagination support

inputFields.name: page
inputFields.description:
Page number for pagination, starting from 1.

    Used to navigate through multiple pages of results when the total number of order statuses exceeds the offset limit. Defaults to 1.

inputFields.name: offset
inputFields.description:
Number of order statuses to return per page.

    Controls the size of each page in the paginated response to manage data volume and performance. Must be between 1 and 100. Defaults to 12.

inputFields.name: ids
inputFields.description: List of order status ids

inputFields.name: codes
inputFields.description: List of order status codes

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description:
Filter by order status name using partial text matching.

    Supports case-insensitive search to find order statuses with names containing the specified text, useful for finding specific workflow states.

inputFields.name: priority
inputFields.description:
Filter by priority value for workflow ordering.

    Searches for order statuses with the specified priority level. Lower numbers indicate higher priority in workflow displays and processing sequences.

inputFields.name: type
inputFields.description:
Filter by order status type classification.

    SYSTEM types are built-in platform statuses that cannot be modified, while CUSTOM types are user-defined statuses for specific business workflows.

inputFields.name: orderType
inputFields.description:
Filter by the type of orders this status applies to.

    Determines which business processes can use this status: quotations for price quotes, orders for confirmed purchases, or requests for special inquiries.

inputFields.name: createdAt
inputFields.description:
Filter by creation date range.

    Allows searching for order statuses created within a specific date range using greater than, less than, or between date criteria.

inputFields.name: lastModifiedAt
inputFields.description:
Filter by last modification date range.

    Allows searching for order statuses modified within a specific date range using greater than, less than, or between date criteria.

inputFields.name: isDefault
inputFields.description:
Filter by default status flag.

    When true, returns only statuses marked as default for new orders. When false, returns only non-default statuses.

inputFields.name: isPublic
inputFields.description: Filter by public visibility flag.

inputFields.name: isEditable
inputFields.description:
Filter by editable flag.

    When true, returns only statuses that can be modified. When false, returns only locked statuses that cannot be changed.

inputFields.name: isDeletable
inputFields.description:
Filter by deletable flag.

    When true, returns only statuses that can be removed. When false, returns only protected statuses that cannot be deleted.

inputFields.name: isExportable
inputFields.description: Marks if the order is exportable

inputFields.name: isConfirmable
inputFields.description: Marks if the order is confirmable

inputFields.name: isArchivable
inputFields.description: Marks if the order can be archived

inputFields.name: sortInputs
inputFields.description:
Sorting configuration for the search results.

    Allows multiple sort criteria to be applied in order of priority. Each sort input specifies a field and direction for ordering the results.

types.name: OrderStatusOrderType
types.description: Classification of order types that determine which workflow statuses are applicable for different business processes

enumValues.name: QUOTATION
enumValues.description: Order status applicable to quotation processes. Used for managing price quotes, estimates, and preliminary order proposals before final confirmation.

enumValues.name: ORDER
enumValues.description: Order status applicable to confirmed orders. Used for managing the complete order lifecycle from confirmation through fulfillment and delivery.

enumValues.name: REQUEST
enumValues.description: Order status applicable to request processes. Used for managing special requests, custom orders, or inquiry-based transactions that require additional processing.

fields.name: id
fields.description:
Unique identifier for the order status set.

    Used for referencing this status set in API calls, workflow configurations, and order status assignments.

fields.name: name
fields.description:
Human-readable name for the order status set.

    Used for display purposes in user interfaces and workflow management. Provides clear identification of the status set for business users.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
Detailed description of the order status set purpose and usage.

    Provides additional context about the workflow this set represents and when it should be used for order management.

fields.name: orderStatuses
fields.description:
Collection of order statuses contained within this set.

    Represents all the possible statuses that orders can have when using this workflow configuration. The order and relationships between these statuses define the complete workflow process.

    Must be an exact match for an existing order status set name in the system. Maximum length is 64 characters.

    Controls whether results are ordered in ascending (ASC) or descending (DESC) order. Defaults to ascending order.

    Comma-separated list of numeric IDs to retrieve multiple specific status sets in a single request. Each ID must be unique and represent a valid status set.

types.name: OrderStatusSortField

enumValues.name: ID

enumValues.name: NAME
enumValues.description: Sort alphabetically by order status name

enumValues.name: CODE
enumValues.description: Sort alphabetically by order status code

enumValues.name: PRIORITY
enumValues.description: Sort by priority value, with lower numbers indicating higher priority

enumValues.name: LAST_MODIFIED_AT
enumValues.description: Sort by last modification timestamp, most recent first or last

enumValues.name: CREATED_AT
enumValues.description: Sort by creation timestamp, newest first or oldest first

types.name: OrderStatusSortInput

inputFields.name: field
inputFields.description:
Field to sort the order status results by.

    Determines which property of the order status will be used for ordering the search results. Available options include ID, name, code, priority, and timestamps.

inputFields.name: order
inputFields.description:
Sort direction for the specified field.

    Controls whether results are ordered in ascending (ASC) or descending (DESC) order. Defaults to ascending order.

enumValues.name: CUSTOM
enumValues.description: User-defined order status created for specific business workflows. These statuses can be customized, modified, and deleted according to business requirements.

inputFields.name: phone
inputFields.description: Address fixed phone number [min: 6, max: 20]

inputFields.name: mobile

inputFields.name: email
inputFields.description: Address email [valid email format required]

inputFields.name: code
inputFields.description: Internal code or reference for this address

inputFields.name: notes
inputFields.description: Additional notes about this address

inputFields.name: icp
inputFields.description: ICP address field defines whether tax should be applied to an order if the address is selected as a delivery address. Used when shipping b2b order abroad

inputFields.name: userId
inputFields.description:
Identifier of the user who placed the order.

References the customer account that initiated this order. Can be null for guest orders or orders placed on behalf of customers.

inputFields.name: accountManagerId
inputFields.description:
Identifier of the account manager responsible for this order.

References the staff member or owner who will handle order processing, customer communication, and order fulfillment coordination.

inputFields.name: channelId
inputFields.description:
Sales channel identifier where the order originated.

Distinguishes between different sales channels like webshop, mobile app, marketplace, or point-of-sale systems for reporting and processing rules.

inputFields.name: debtorId
inputFields.description:
Financial identifier of the customer or company responsible for payment.

Used in accounting and billing systems to identify the party responsible for payment and invoice generation.

inputFields.name: debtorFirstName
inputFields.description: First name of the Debtor [USED FOR ORDER SEARCH ONLY]

inputFields.name: debtorLastName
inputFields.description: Last name of the Debtor [USED FOR ORDER SEARCH ONLY]

inputFields.name: debtorCompany
inputFields.description: Company name of the Debtor [USED FOR ORDER SEARCH ONLY]

inputFields.name: recipientFirstName
inputFields.description: First name of the Recipient [USED FOR ORDER SEARCH ONLY]

inputFields.name: recipientLastName
inputFields.description: Last name of the Recipient [USED FOR ORDER SEARCH ONLY]

inputFields.name: recipientCompany
inputFields.description: Company name of the Recipient [USED FOR ORDER SEARCH ONLY]

inputFields.name: externalId
inputFields.description: [DEPRECATED] ID of the Order in an external system

inputFields.name: status
inputFields.description: Status of the Order

inputFields.name: type
inputFields.description: Type of the Order

inputFields.name: source
inputFields.description: Source of the Order

inputFields.name: email
inputFields.description: Email address used for communication about this Order

inputFields.name: remarks
inputFields.description: Remarks by the customer

inputFields.name: reference
inputFields.description: Reference by the customer

inputFields.name: extra3
inputFields.description:
Custom metadata field for order extensions.

Open text field for storing additional order information that can be used in frontend displays or system integrations. Not visible to system administrators but available for custom implementations.

inputFields.name: extra4
inputFields.description:
Additional custom metadata field for order extensions.

Second open text field for storing supplementary order information for frontend customization or integration purposes. Provides flexibility for custom order data requirements.

inputFields.name: currency
inputFields.description: Order currency - ISO 4217 currency code => https://www.xe.com/iso4217.php

inputFields.name: currencyRatio
inputFields.description: Currency conversion ratio compared the Order's base currency

inputFields.name: language
inputFields.description: Order language - ISO 639-1 language code => https://localizely.com/iso-639-1-list/

inputFields.name: actionCode
inputFields.description: Active actioncode that was applied to this Order

inputFields.name: invoiceUserId
inputFields.description:
Invoice recipient identifier.

Specifies the user, contact, or customer who should receive the invoice for this order, which may differ from the person who placed the order.

inputFields.name: validUntil
inputFields.description:
Order validity expiration.

Date and time until which the order remains valid, particularly relevant for quotations and orders with time-sensitive pricing or availability.

inputFields.name: companyId
inputFields.description: ID of the Company of the Order

inputFields.name: companySource
inputFields.description: A company source

inputFields.name: originalOrderId
inputFields.description:
Source order reference for related orders.

Links to the original order when this order is a modification, return, or related transaction, maintaining order relationship history.

inputFields.name: exportedAt
inputFields.description:
External system export timestamp.

Date and time when the order was last exported to external systems like ERP, accounting software, or fulfillment systems.

inputFields.name: exportStatus
inputFields.description:
External system synchronization status.

Indicates the current state of order export to external systems, tracking successful exports, failures, or pending synchronization.

inputFields.name: exportMessage
inputFields.description:
Export process status message.

Detailed message about the export process, including success confirmations or error descriptions for troubleshooting integration issues.

inputFields.name: sources
inputFields.description:
List of order sources

Identifies how the order was created in the system.

inputFields.name: items
inputFields.description: The OrderItem to create or update with the Order as a single bulk request

inputFields.name: paymentData
inputFields.description: Order's payment data

inputFields.name: postageData
inputFields.description: Order's postage data

inputFields.name: total
inputFields.description: Order's totals, tax and global discount

inputFields.name: addresses
inputFields.description: Expected 1 invoice and/or 1 delivery type of address

types.name: Orient
types.description:
The orient parameter controls the cardinal orientation of the image.
    
  NOTE: 
  - By default, if the source image contains orientation information stored within its metadata, that orientation will be applied to the image data and the orientation override removed from metadata.
 - The numerical values are the same as EXIF rotation numbers.

enumValues.name: RIGHT
enumValues.description: Orient the image right.

enumValues.name: LEFT
enumValues.description: Orient the image left.

enumValues.name: HORIZONTAL_FLIP
enumValues.description: Flip the image horizontally.

enumValues.name: VERTICAL_FLIP
enumValues.description: Flip the image horizontally.

enumValues.name: HV_FLIP
enumValues.description: Flip the image both horizontally and vertically (also vh).

enumValues.name: ONE
enumValues.description: Default - don't do anything...

enumValues.name: TWO
enumValues.description: Flip the image horizontally.

enumValues.name: THREE
enumValues.description: Flip the image horizontally and vertically.

enumValues.name: FOUR
enumValues.description: Flip the image vertically.

enumValues.name: FIVE
enumValues.description: Flip the image horizontally, then orient the image left (also rv or vr).

enumValues.name: SIX
enumValues.description: Orient the image right.

enumValues.name: SEVEN
enumValues.description: Flip the image horizontally, then orient the image right (also lv or vl).

enumValues.name: EIGHT
enumValues.description: Orient the image left.

types.name: PadInput
types.description:
Add pixels to the edge of an image.

Notes
- By default padding applies a background color of white. See bg-color to change that.
- If the source image contains a transparent background and the output image also contains transparency, the padding will be made up of transparent pixels.
- When using pad and canvas at the same time, pad will be ignored.
- Values can be specified using CSS style shorthand values. (see CSS Margin or CSS Padding for more examples).
- CSS shorthand allows for all edges to be specified in one property.
- Any fractional pixel measurements will be rounded to the nearest whole pixel.

inputFields.name: top
inputFields.description: Top - Pixels or Percentage - Default=0

inputFields.name: right
inputFields.description: Right - Pixels or Percentage - Default=0

inputFields.name: bottom
inputFields.description: Bottom - Pixels or Percentage - Default=0

inputFields.name: left
inputFields.description: Left - Pixels or Percentage - Default=0

types.name: PaginatedMediaAttachmentResponse
types.description: Paginated response containing private attachment files with navigation metadata

fields.name: items
fields.description:
Collection of items returned for the current page.

      Contains the actual data items matching the search criteria, limited by the pagination settings. The array will be empty if no items match the search criteria.

fields.name: itemsFound
fields.description:
Total number of items matching the search criteria.

      Represents the complete count of items that match the search filters, regardless of pagination. Used to calculate total pages and display result counts.

fields.name: offset
fields.description:
Number of items displayed per page.

      Controls how many items are returned in each page of results. Used for pagination calculations and determining page boundaries.

fields.name: page
fields.description:
Current page number being displayed.

      Indicates which page of results is currently being shown. Pages start at 1 and increment based on the offset size.

fields.name: pages
fields.description:
Total number of pages available.

      Calculated based on the total items found divided by the items per page. Used for pagination navigation and determining if there are more pages available.

fields.name: start
fields.description:
Starting position number for items on the current page.

      Indicates the position of the first item displayed on this page within the complete result set. Useful for displaying "Showing X-Y of Z results" information.

fields.name: end
fields.description:
Ending position number for items on the current page.

      Indicates the position of the last item displayed on this page within the complete result set. Will not exceed the total number of items found.

types.name: PasswordRecoveryLinkInput
types.description:
Parameters for generating password recovery links and sending password reset emails.

  Contains user identification and customization options for the password recovery process.

inputFields.name: email
inputFields.description:
Email address of the user requesting password recovery.

    Must be a valid email address associated with an existing user account in the system.

inputFields.name: redirectUrl
inputFields.description:
URL to redirect the user after successful password reset.

    The user will be redirected to this URL after completing the password reset process. If not provided, a default redirect will be used.

inputFields.name: language
inputFields.description:
Language code for localized password recovery communications.

    Two-character language code (ISO 639-1) used to determine the language for password recovery emails and interfaces. Must be exactly 2 characters long.

inputFields.name: channelId
inputFields.description: The channel id to send the password reset email to. If not provided, the email will be sent to the default channel.

types.name: PasswordResetLinkEmailInput
types.description:
Password reset email configuration for user account recovery.

Extends email event input with specific fields needed for password reset functionality, including user identification, redirect handling, and localization options.

inputFields.name: type
inputFields.description:
Classification of the email event being triggered.

Determines the purpose and context of the email, enabling appropriate template selection and content personalization based on the business scenario.

inputFields.name: siteId
inputFields.description:
Legacy site identifier for email context.

Previously used to determine site-specific email configuration, now deprecated as site context is derived from other inputs automatically.

inputFields.name: orderId
inputFields.description: The id of the order to use when triggering an event of type orderconfirm

inputFields.name: userId
inputFields.description:
User identifier for recipient determination.

Used to identify the email recipient when the user cannot be derived from other context inputs like order or contact information.

inputFields.name: letterId
inputFields.description:
Campaign letter identifier for marketing emails.

Required when triggering campaign-type events, references the specific marketing letter or campaign content to be sent.

inputFields.name: subject
inputFields.description:
Custom email subject line override.

Provides a specific subject line when it cannot be derived from template configuration or other context inputs, enabling dynamic subject customization.

inputFields.name: content
inputFields.description:
Custom email content override.

Provides specific email content when it cannot be derived from template configuration or other context inputs, enabling dynamic content customization.

inputFields.name: from
inputFields.description:
Email sender information override.

Specifies the email sender when it cannot be derived from template configuration or other context inputs, enabling flexible sender customization.

inputFields.name: attachments
inputFields.description:
File attachments for the email.

Base64-encoded files that will be attached to the email message, supporting various file types for comprehensive communication needs.

inputFields.name: variables
inputFields.description:
Template variable data for dynamic content.

JSON object containing key-value pairs that will be used for Handlebars variable substitution in the email template, enabling personalized and context-specific content generation.

Validation: Must be valid JSON format.

inputFields.name: language
inputFields.description:
Language code for email localization.

ISO 639-1 language code determining which language version of the password reset email template to use.

Validation: Must be exactly 2 characters long.

inputFields.name: email
inputFields.description:
User email address for password reset.

Valid email address of the user account that requires password reset, must match an existing user account in the system.

Validation: Must be a valid email format.

inputFields.name: redirectUrl
inputFields.description:
Post-reset redirect destination URL.

The page URL where the user will be redirected after successfully changing their password, enabling seamless user experience and proper application flow.

inputFields.name: linkText
inputFields.description:
Custom link text for the reset button.

Text that will be displayed on the password reset link or button in the email, allowing customization of the call-to-action message.

types.name: Payment
types.description:
Payment record representing a financial transaction for order processing.

Manages payment lifecycle from creation through completion, including amount tracking, currency handling, and payment method processing. Supports multiple payment states and transaction history for comprehensive financial management.

fields.name: id
fields.description:
Unique system identifier for the payment record.

Primary key used to reference this payment throughout the system and for payment tracking operations.

fields.name: userId
fields.description:
Authenticated user identifier for the payment.

References the logged-in user who initiated this payment. Used for user-specific payment history and account management. Must be a positive integer when provided.

fields.name: anonymousId
fields.description:
Anonymous user identifier for guest payments.

References the guest user session for payments made without authentication. Used for guest checkout tracking and order management. Must be a positive integer when provided.

fields.name: paymentId
fields.description:
External payment service provider identifier.

Unique payment reference provided by the Payment Service Provider (PSP) for tracking and reconciliation. Used for payment status updates and transaction matching. Maximum length of 255 characters. Must be unique across all payments.

fields.name: orderId
fields.description:
Order identifier associated with this payment.

References the specific order that this payment is intended to fulfill. Used for order-payment relationship tracking and fulfillment processing. Must be a positive integer.

fields.name: amount
fields.description:
Payment amount in smallest currency unit (cents).

The total amount to be paid expressed in the smallest denomination of the currency (e.g., cents for USD, pence for GBP). Used for precise financial calculations. Must be a positive integer.

fields.name: currency
fields.description:
Payment currency code in ISO 4217 format.

Three-letter currency code (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP) following ISO 4217 standard. Used for currency conversion and financial reporting. Must be exactly 3 uppercase letters.

fields.name: method
fields.description:
Payment method identifier used by the payment processor.

Specific payment method code used by the Payment Service Provider for processing this transaction (e.g., 'credit_card', 'paypal', 'bank_transfer'). Maximum length of 100 characters.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Current payment processing status

fields.name: transactions
fields.description: Collection of transactions associated with this payment for detailed financial tracking

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: Timestamp when the payment record was initially created

fields.name: createdBy
fields.description: Identifier of the user who created the payment record

fields.name: lastModifiedAt
fields.description: Timestamp when the payment record was last modified

fields.name: lastModifiedBy
fields.description: Identifier of the user who last modified the payment record

types.name: PaymentsResponse
types.description:
Paginated response containing payment records with navigation metadata.

Includes payment data along with pagination controls for efficient data browsing and client-side state management. Supports response expansion to include related transaction details.

fields.name: items
fields.description: List of items of type Payment

fields.name: itemsFound
fields.description: Total number of items found

fields.name: offset
fields.description: The amount of items to show per page

fields.name: page
fields.description: The current page

fields.name: pages
fields.description: Total amount of pages

fields.name: start
fields.description: Start position of the current page

fields.name: end
fields.description: End position of the current page

types.name: PaymentsSearchInput
types.description:
Search and pagination parameters for retrieving payment records.

Controls the pagination behavior and response expansion options for payment queries. Provides efficient data retrieval with configurable page sizes and optional related data inclusion.

inputFields.name: page
inputFields.description:
Page number for pagination navigation starting from 1.

Specifies which page of results to retrieve when the total number of payments exceeds the offset limit. Minimum value is 1. Defaults to 1 if not specified.

inputFields.name: offset
inputFields.description:
Number of items per page for controlling response size.

Controls the size of each page in the paginated response to manage data volume and performance. Minimum value is 1, maximum value is 100. Defaults to 12 if not specified.

types.name: PaymentStatuses
types.description: Payment processing status indicating the current stage of payment lifecycle and available business actions

enumValues.name: OPEN
enumValues.description: Payment created but not yet processed by payment service provider - awaiting initial processing

enumValues.name: PENDING
enumValues.description: Payment submitted to payment service provider and awaiting processing response

enumValues.name: AUTHORIZED
enumValues.description: Payment authorized but funds not yet captured - authorization can be captured or cancelled

enumValues.name: CANCELLED
enumValues.description: Payment cancelled before completion - no funds were processed

enumValues.name: EXPIRED
enumValues.description: Payment authorization expired without capture - funds are no longer reserved

enumValues.name: FAILED
enumValues.description: Payment processing failed due to insufficient funds, declined card, or other payment issues

enumValues.name: PAID
enumValues.description: Payment successfully completed and funds captured - order can be fulfilled

enumValues.name: REFUNDED
enumValues.description: Payment refunded partially or fully to customer - funds returned to original payment method

enumValues.name: CHARGEBACK
enumValues.description: Payment disputed and charged back by customer's financial institution - funds forcibly reversed

types.name: PayMethod
types.description:
Payment method configuration for checkout and payment processing.

  Represents a payment option available to customers during checkout including configuration details, branding, and integration settings. Used for payment processing, checkout flows, and financial transaction management.

fields.name: id
fields.description:
Unique identifier for the payment method.

    Primary key used to reference this payment method throughout the system for payment processing operations and checkout workflows.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description:
Payment method creation timestamp.

    Date and time when this payment method was initially created in the system, used for auditing and payment method lifecycle tracking.

fields.name: lastModifiedAt
fields.description:
Payment method last modification timestamp.

    Date and time when any payment method configuration was last updated, used for change tracking and synchronization purposes.

fields.name: names
fields.description:
Localized payment method names for different languages.

    Collection of payment method names in various languages for international checkout experiences and multilingual storefronts. Each language must be unique within the collection.

fields.name: externalCode
fields.description:
External system identifier for payment processing integration.

    Unique code used to identify this payment method in external payment processors, gateways, and financial systems. Must follow SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE format and be unique across all payment methods. Maximum 64 characters.

fields.name: logo
fields.description:
Payment method logo URL for checkout display.

    URL pointing to the payment method's logo or icon, used for visual identification in checkout interfaces and payment selection screens. Maximum 255 characters.

types.name: PayMethodCreateInput
types.description:
Input for creating a new payment method.

  Contains all necessary information to establish a new payment method including localized names, external system integration codes, and branding elements. Used for setting up new payment options in checkout flows.

inputFields.name: names
inputFields.description:
Localized payment method names for different languages.

    Collection of payment method names in various languages for international checkout experiences. Each language must be unique and follow ISO 639-1 format. At least one localized name is required.

inputFields.name: externalCode
inputFields.description:
External system identifier in SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE format.

    Unique code used to identify this payment method in external payment processors and gateways. Must follow SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE convention and be unique across all payment methods. Maximum 64 characters.

inputFields.name: logo
inputFields.description:
Payment method logo URL for checkout display.

    Optional URL pointing to the payment method's logo or icon for visual identification in checkout interfaces. Must be a valid URL format with maximum 255 characters.

types.name: PayMethodSearchInput
types.description:
Search criteria for filtering payment methods with comprehensive options.

  Provides flexible search capabilities for payment method discovery including date range filtering, identifier-based searches, and custom sorting. Supports pagination for large payment method catalogs.

inputFields.name: page
inputFields.description:
Page number for pagination.

    Specifies which page of results to return. Must be a positive integer (minimum value: 1). Default is page 1.

inputFields.name: offset
inputFields.description:
Number of items per page.

    Controls how many payment methods are returned in each page. Must be a positive integer (minimum value: 1). Default is 12 payment methods per page.

inputFields.name: createdAt
inputFields.description:
Filter by payment method creation date range.

    Allows filtering payment methods based on when they were originally created in the system. Useful for finding recently added payment methods.

inputFields.name: lastModifiedAt
inputFields.description:
Filter by payment method last modification date range.

    Allows filtering payment methods based on when they were last updated. Useful for finding recently modified payment methods or tracking changes.

inputFields.name: ids
inputFields.description:
Filter by specific payment method identifiers.

    Returns only payment methods whose IDs match the provided list. Each ID must be a positive integer (minimum value: 1) and unique within the array. Useful for retrieving specific payment methods or bulk operations.

inputFields.name: externalCodes
inputFields.description:
Filter by external system codes.

    Returns payment methods matching any of the specified external codes used for payment processor integration. Each code must follow SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE format (pattern: ^([A-Z]*_?[A-Z]*)*$), have maximum length of 64 characters, and be unique within the array.

inputFields.name: sortInputs
inputFields.description:
Sorting configuration for result ordering.

    Defines how the payment method results should be sorted, supporting multiple sort criteria with different directions (ascending/descending). Each sort input must be unique within the array.

types.name: PayMethodUpdateInput
types.description:
Input for updating an existing payment method with partial data.

  Allows partial updates of payment method properties including localized names, external integration codes, and branding elements. Only provided fields will be updated, others remain unchanged.

  Validation constraints:
  - Names array must contain unique languages if provided (minimum 1 item)
  - External code must follow SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE format and be unique (maximum 64 characters)
  - Logo URL must be valid if provided (maximum 255 characters)

inputFields.name: names
inputFields.description:
Localized payment method names for different languages.

    Collection of payment method names in various languages for international checkout experiences. Each language must be unique and follow ISO 639-1 format. At least one localized name is required.

inputFields.name: externalCode
inputFields.description:
External system identifier in SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE format.

    Unique code used to identify this payment method in external payment processors and gateways. Must follow SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE convention and be unique across all payment methods. Maximum 64 characters.

inputFields.name: logo
inputFields.description:
Payment method logo URL for checkout display.

    Optional URL pointing to the payment method's logo or icon for visual identification in checkout interfaces. Must be a valid URL format with maximum 255 characters.

types.name: PriceCalculateInput
types.description:
Price calculation parameters for comprehensive product pricing with customer context.

  Contains all necessary information to determine the appropriate pricing including customer identification, product details, quantity, and geographic context. Used for real-time price calculations and pricing displays.

inputFields.name: productId
inputFields.description:
Unique identifier of the product for price calculation.

    Must be a valid product ID that exists in the system. Used to retrieve base pricing, cost information, and applicable discounts for the specified product.

inputFields.name: quantity
inputFields.description:
Quantity of the product for price calculation.

    Minimum value is 1. Used to determine volume discounts, bulk pricing tiers, and quantity-based promotional offers. Higher quantities may result in better pricing.

inputFields.name: taxZone
inputFields.description:
Geographic tax zone for tax calculations and regional pricing.

    Must be a valid 2-character country code. Used to determine applicable tax rates, regional pricing rules, and zone-specific discounts. Defaults to "NL" if not specified.

inputFields.name: contactId
inputFields.description:
Contact identifier for personalized pricing calculations.

    When provided, enables contact-specific pricing, pricesheet assignments, and personalized discounts. Cannot be used together with customerId. Use 0 or omit for anonymous pricing.

types.name: PriceDiscountType
types.description: The type of discount calculation used to calculate the price for a product

enumValues.name: COST_PRICE_PLUS
enumValues.description: Cost price plus a percentage

enumValues.name: LIST_PRICE_MIN
enumValues.description: List price minus a percentage

enumValues.name: NET_PRICE
enumValues.description: Fixed amount

types.name: PriceDisplay
types.description:
Frontend price display configuration options for user interface presentation.

  Defines how prices should be formatted and displayed in customer-facing interfaces. Each option provides specific formatting rules for different pricing scenarios and promotional displays.

enumValues.name: FROM
enumValues.description:
Display starting price format.

      Shows "Price from [Cheapest price]" format, typically used for products with multiple pricing tiers or volume discounts to indicate the lowest available price.

enumValues.name: FROM_FOR
enumValues.description:
Display promotional pricing format with old and new prices.

      Shows "Old price [Suggested price] new price [Sale price]" format, used for promotional displays to highlight savings and price reductions.

enumValues.name: LISTPRICE_OURPRICE
enumValues.description:
Display comparative pricing format.

      Shows "List price [Suggested price] our price [Sale price]" format, used to compare manufacturer's suggested price with the actual selling price.

enumValues.name: PACKAGE
enumValues.description:
Display package-based pricing format.

      Shows "Price [Sale price/Order unit] per [Order UOM]" format, used for products sold in packages or bulk quantities to show per-unit pricing.

enumValues.name: PER_UOM
enumValues.description:
Display unit of measure pricing format.

      Shows "Price from [Cheapest price/Package unit] per [Package UOM]" format, used to display pricing per unit of measure for comparison shopping.

enumValues.name: ON_REQUEST
enumValues.description:
Display price on request format.

      Shows "Price on request" message instead of actual pricing, used for custom products, high-value items, or when pricing requires consultation.

enumValues.name: DEFAULT
enumValues.description:
Standard price display format.

      Uses the default pricing display without special formatting. Shows the standard price information as configured in the base price settings.

types.name: PriceElementType
types.description:
Classification of price elements used in pricing calculations and display logic.

  Defines the type of pricing component being processed, enabling proper handling of different price sources and calculation methods in the pricing engine.

enumValues.name: BULK_COST_PRICE
enumValues.description:
Volume-based cost pricing tier.

      Represents cost prices that apply to specific quantity ranges, enabling volume purchasing advantages and margin calculations based on purchase quantities.

enumValues.name: BULK_SALES_PRICE
enumValues.description:
Volume-based sales pricing tier.

      Represents sales prices that apply to specific quantity ranges, enabling volume discounts and tiered pricing strategies for customer purchases.

enumValues.name: PRICESHEET
enumValues.description:
Pricesheet-based pricing context.

      Represents pricing derived from pricesheet assignments, enabling customer-specific pricing and channel-based pricing strategies.

enumValues.name: DEFAULT
enumValues.description:
Default base pricing.

      Represents standard pricing without special conditions, used as fallback when no specific pricing rules apply or as base price for calculations.

types.name: PriceFieldName
types.description: Price field name

enumValues.name: PRODUCT_ID
enumValues.description: [Optional][Integer] An id of a product. Use this or the combination of SOURCE_ID and SOURCE_NAME.

enumValues.name: PER
enumValues.description:
[Optional][Integer] Unit quantity for the listed price.

Defines how many units of the product are included in the listed price. For example, a value of 1 means the price is per single unit, while 12 might indicate the price is per dozen.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description:
[Required][Number][5 Decimal Places] Base selling price for the product.

This is the standard price used for pricing calculations before applying customer-specific discounts, volume pricing, or promotional offers. Serves as the foundation for all pricing logic.

enumValues.name: STORE
enumValues.description:
[Optional][Number][5 Decimal Places] Physical store selling price.

The price at which the product is sold in brick-and-mortar stores. May differ from online prices due to store-specific costs, regional pricing strategies, or channel-specific promotions.

enumValues.name: COST
enumValues.description:
[Optional][Number][5 Decimal Places] Product acquisition cost for margin calculations.

The cost incurred to acquire or produce the product, used for profit margin analysis, pricing decisions, and financial reporting. Essential for maintaining profitable pricing strategies.

enumValues.name: SUGGESTED
enumValues.description:
[Optional][Number][5 Decimal Places] Manufacturer's recommended retail price (RRP).

The price recommended by the manufacturer or supplier for retail sales. Used as a reference point for pricing decisions and can be displayed to customers as a comparison price.

enumValues.name: BULK_DISCOUNT_TYPE
enumValues.description:
[Optional][Enum][costpriceplus, listpricemin, netprice]. Volume discount calculation method.

Defines how bulk discounts are applied: as a percentage reduction from the base price or as a fixed discounted price per unit. Determines the calculation logic for volume pricing. Default is listpricemin

enumValues.name: DEFAULT_TAX_CODE
enumValues.description:
[Required][Enum][H, L, N, M, CUST]. Standard tax classification for the product.

The default tax code applied to this product for tax calculations. Can be overridden by zone-specific tax codes or customer-specific tax settings. Default is H

enumValues.name: DISPLAY
enumValues.description:
[Optional][Enum][FROM, FROM_FOR, LISTPRICE_OURPRICE, PACKAGE, PER_UOM, ON_REQUEST, DEFAULT]. Frontend price display configuration.

Controls how the price should be presented in user interfaces. Options include standard display, promotional formatting, or special pricing indicators. Default value is 'DEFAULT'.

enumValues.name: SOURCE_ID
enumValues.description: [Optional][String] The id of the source. Use in combination with SOURCE_NAME, or alternatively use PRODUCT_ID.

enumValues.name: SOURCE_NAME
enumValues.description: [Optional][String] The name of the source. Use in combination with SOURCE_ID, or alternatively use PRODUCT_ID.

enumValues.name: PLATFORM
enumValues.description: Price is calculated based on product price/postage rules set in Propeller.

enumValues.name: EXTERNAL
enumValues.description: Price is set externally

types.name: PriceSearchInput
types.description:
Search criteria for filtering price records.

  Provides comprehensive filtering options for price searches including product associations, price ranges, effective dates, and pricesheet assignments. Supports pagination and sorting for efficient data management.

inputFields.name: page
inputFields.description:
Page number for pagination.

    Specifies which page of results to return. Must be 1 or greater.

inputFields.name: offset
inputFields.description:
Number of items per page.

    Controls how many price records are returned in each page. Must be 1 or greater.

inputFields.name: createdAt
inputFields.description:
Filter by price creation date range.

    Allows filtering prices based on when they were originally created. Useful for finding prices created within specific time periods.

inputFields.name: lastModifiedAt
inputFields.description:
Filter by last modification date range.

    Allows filtering prices based on when they were last updated. Useful for finding recently modified prices or tracking pricing changes.

inputFields.name: ids
inputFields.description:
Filter by specific price record identifiers.

    Allows searching for specific price records using their unique UUID identifiers. Useful for retrieving multiple known price records in a single query.

inputFields.name: productIds
inputFields.description:
Filter by product identifiers.

    Allows searching for price records associated with specific products. Useful for retrieving pricing information for multiple products in a single query.

inputFields.name: per
inputFields.description:
Filter by unit quantity for pricing.

    Searches for prices with a specific 'per' value, which defines how many units are included in the listed price. For example, searching for 'per: 12' finds prices that are set per dozen.

inputFields.name: list
inputFields.description:
Filter by list price range.

    Searches for prices within specified list price ranges. List price is the base selling price used for pricing calculations before applying discounts or promotions.

inputFields.name: cost
inputFields.description:
Filter by cost price range.

    Searches for prices within specified cost price ranges. Cost price represents the acquisition cost for margin calculations and pricing decisions.

inputFields.name: suggested
inputFields.description:
Filter by suggested price range.

    Searches for prices within specified suggested price ranges. Suggested price is the manufacturer's recommended retail price (RRP) used as a reference point for pricing decisions.

inputFields.name: store
inputFields.description:
Filter by store price range.

    Searches for prices within specified store price ranges. Store price is the selling price in physical stores, which may differ from online prices due to channel-specific strategies.

inputFields.name: bulkDiscountType
inputFields.description:
Filter by bulk discount calculation method.

    Searches for prices with a specific bulk discount type. Determines how volume discounts are calculated and applied to bulk purchases.

inputFields.name: sortInputs
inputFields.description:
Sorting configuration for search results.

    Defines how the returned price records should be ordered. Multiple sort criteria can be specified to create complex sorting logic.

types.name: Pricesheet
types.description:
Pricesheet entity for customer-specific pricing strategies and discount management.

  Represents a collection of pricing rules, discounts, and customer assignments that define how products are priced for specific customer segments, regions, or business scenarios.

fields.name: id
fields.description:
Unique identifier for the pricesheet record.

    Primary key used for pricesheet management and customer assignment operations.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description:
Timestamp when the pricesheet record was initially created.

    Used for audit trails and pricesheet lifecycle management.

fields.name: lastModifiedAt
fields.description:
Timestamp when the pricesheet record was last updated.

    Essential for tracking pricesheet changes and synchronization.

fields.name: code
fields.description:
Unique pricesheet code for identification and reference.

    Human-readable identifier used for pricesheet management and integration with external systems. Must be unique within the tenant. Maximum length is 64 characters.

fields.name: names
fields.description:
Localized display names for the pricesheet.

    Multi-language support for pricesheet names, allowing different names for different locales. Used for user interface display and customer-facing documentation.

fields.name: descriptions
fields.description:
Localized descriptions for the pricesheet.

    Multi-language support for detailed pricesheet descriptions, providing context and usage information in different locales. Used for documentation and customer communication.

fields.name: priority
fields.description:
Pricesheet priority for conflict resolution.

    Numeric priority used when multiple pricesheets apply to the same customer or scenario. Higher numbers indicate higher priority. Default value is 1. Minimum value is 1.

fields.name: readonly
fields.description:
Read-only status preventing modifications.

    When true, the pricesheet cannot be modified through standard operations. Used for protecting system-generated or finalized pricesheets from accidental changes.

fields.name: contacts
fields.description: Use contactsPaginated.

fields.name: customers
fields.description: Use customersPaginated.

fields.name: companies
fields.description: Use companiesPaginated.

inputFields.description: Customer ids

inputFields.description: Usergroups

types.name: PricesheetCreateInput
types.description:
Pricesheet creation data for establishing pricing strategies and customer segment management.

  Contains essential information to create pricesheet records including identification codes, localized names and descriptions, priority settings, and access controls. Pricesheets define pricing rules and customer assignments for targeted pricing strategies.

inputFields.name: code
inputFields.description:
Unique identifier code for the pricesheet.

    Alphanumeric code used to identify and reference the pricesheet across the system. Must be unique and is used for pricesheet management and customer assignments.

inputFields.name: names
inputFields.description:
Localized display names for the pricesheet in multiple languages.

    Array of language-specific names that provide user-friendly display text for the pricesheet. Each entry must have a unique language code and corresponding display value.

inputFields.name: descriptions
inputFields.description:
Localized descriptions for the pricesheet in multiple languages.

    Array of language-specific descriptions that provide detailed information about the pricesheet's purpose and usage. Each entry must have a unique language code and corresponding description text.

inputFields.name: priority
inputFields.description:
Priority level for pricesheet application order.

    Integer value determining the order in which pricesheets are evaluated when multiple pricesheets apply to the same customer. Higher values indicate higher priority.

inputFields.name: readonly
inputFields.description:
Read-only access control flag for pricesheet protection.

    When set to true, allows front-end to prevent modification or deletion of the pricesheet. Used to protect critical pricing configurations from accidental changes.

types.name: PricesheetsEffectiveInput
types.description:
Input parameters for retrieving effective pricesheets for a customer or contact.

  Retrieves all pricesheets that apply to a specific user, including directly assigned pricesheets and pricesheets linked via usergroups, ordered by priority.

inputFields.name: contactId
inputFields.description:
Contact identifier to retrieve effective pricesheets for.

    Cannot be used together with customerId. When provided, retrieves all pricesheets assigned to this contact, their company (if companyId is specified or default company), and their usergroups.

inputFields.name: companyId
inputFields.description:
Company identifier to retrieve company-specific pricesheets.

    Must be used together with contactId. When omitted, the contact's default company will be used. This parameter is ignored when customerId is provided.

inputFields.name: customerId
inputFields.description:
Customer identifier to retrieve effective pricesheets for.

    Cannot be used together with contactId or companyId. When provided, retrieves all pricesheets assigned to this customer and their usergroups.

inputFields.name: offset
inputFields.description:
Number of pricesheet records to return per page.

    Minimum value is 1. Controls the size of each page in paginated results for efficient data loading.

inputFields.name: page
inputFields.description:
Page number for pagination results.

    Minimum value is 1. Used with offset to control result pagination for large pricesheet datasets.

Number of pricesheet records to return per page.
